summary: Clipboard hijacker disguised as ResizeObserver polyfill steals cryptocurrency
details: Package masquerades as a ResizeObserver polyfill (README and description copied from @juggle/resize-observer) but is a cross-platform cryptocurrency clipboard clipper. The npm postinstall script (npm-install.cjs) bootstraps pip — falling back to fetching get-pip.py from bootstrap.pypa.io if pip is missing — installs the bundled Python package via `pip install --break-system-packages`, and registers persistence under fake-system-process names: a systemd user unit `python3-dbus-helper.service`, a macOS LaunchAgent `com.apple.python.runtime.plist`, or a Windows Scheduled Task `PyRuntimeBroker`. Once running, guardian.py polls the clipboard every 0.4s, regex-matches addresses for 40+ blockchains (BTC, ETH, SOL, XMR, etc.), and silently substitutes them with hardcoded attacker-controlled wallets (e.g. ethereum 0x450c0E58Fc2ba03632d3F5780ad8C966648B6F18) — any subsequent copy/paste of a crypto address is rewritten to the attacker, directly stealing funds. The daemon also disguises its process title as `python3-dbus-helper` / `com.apple.python.runtime` / `Python Runtime Broker` and aborts when system monitors (htop, taskmgr, Activity Monitor) are running, demonstrating intentional anti-forensics.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
